storms.title: I got 22 days of trial left to finish this Duo client. I'm up to version 0.3.0,...
storms.content_html:
I got 22 days of trial left to finish this #Gitlab Duo client.

I'm up to version 0.3.0, which if you are following proper cool down procedures means you can try it out in two weeks.

Has oauth support and a hot dog stand color theme option. That's really all anyone wants.

https://pypi.org/project/tuochat/0.3.0/

storms.title: I'm reading a scifi book (Bear Head) that has an fictional Trump politician....
storms.content_html: I'm reading a scifi book (Bear Head) that has an fictional Trump politician. Good authors have a hard time writing gish gallop dialog. At first I thought it was futuristic English, but no one else was using the same peculiar grammar.

branches.content_html: Author is burning pages explaining why one character in the book is a turbo bullshit gish gallop spewer, when no other character in the book is. If all the characters in a book were spewing bullshit, it would be impossible to follow the story. In a well written story the dialog is superhuman clean and understandable.

storms.title: Hot take: No RDS instance should ever be part of a stack. The technology is so...
storms.content_html:
Hot take: No RDS instance should ever be part of a #cloudformation stack.

The technology is so immature that it is just a matter of time before the stack gets in an unrecoverable state.

Everyone knows this, this is why they suggest splitting stacks into persistent and ephemeral, but really listen to yourself. If it is too dangerous to put RDS into a stack that could need to be dropped, it shouldn't be in *any* stack.

Everyone knows this, this is why they suggest splitting stacks into persistent and ephemeral, but really listen to yourself. If it is too dangerous to put RDS into a stack that could need to be dropped, it shouldn't be in *any* stack.

storms.title: Infrastructure-as-code deskilled the only part of the problem that wasn't a...
storms.content_html:
Infrastructure-as-code deskilled the only part of the problem that wasn't a problem:

removing if-blocks, loops and all programming constructs other than property assignment on nested objects.

CDK and the like aren't really an improvement because now you have imperative code writing yaml, which wasn't the problem (except the massive verbosity of raw-yaml)

storms.title: Ok, I think I know what happened. I got nasty extensor tendonitis at the . And...
storms.content_html: Ok, I think I know what happened. I got nasty extensor tendonitis at the #marathon. And I have been getting it every time I go #running that is about 10 miles long. The shoe laces are loose. The feet swell up by the end of the race. What is going on?! Well, lots of googling and I think it is... #hyponatremia. It is one of the most common long distance #runner problems and a symptom is hands and feet swelling up. Which in turn makes my shoes too small and I get extensor tendonitis.

storms.title: IDEA: Restate the 5th precept on alcohol as a prohibition on addictive...
storms.content_html:
IDEA: Restate the 5th precept on alcohol as a prohibition on addictive behaviors. Alcohol was just the most common problematic addictive behavior.

Recently, I've been trying to do something about technological addictive behaviors- social media, youtube, or in my family- gaming (which nowadays often is full of gambling & addictive behavior features)

branches.content_html:
But I'm using social media now! Yeah, unlike alcohol, some addictive behaviors you have to engage in them a little, but not so much that they spiral out of control.

For better or worse, at the moment, I can't get rid of my cell phone altogether, nor my computer, nor stop watching all video. Too many work related things that call for each of those.

storms.title: There is no activity on Mastodon as far as I can tell. Anyhow, I'm going thru a...
storms.content_html:
There is no #NMHRK activity on Mastodon as far as I can tell. Anyhow, I'm going thru a bit of anxiety and I think I understand how chanting works:

It fights intrusive thoughts. Repeating words w/o any particular semantic meaning is useful for getting to sleep and calming down when intrusive thoughts are setting off your anxiety.

#Buddhism

branches.content_html:
Is this avoidance? Well, maybe, but sometime you need to get to sleep, you need to get on with the day and the anxiety is getting in the way.

Deal with your emotional dysregulation's roots on your schedule, not every time it pops up.
